title: Getting Started documentation uses podman inspect -l, but the option is not available in Podman 6.0.1
body:
### Issue Description

While following the official Getting Started documentation, I noticed that it uses the command:

```sh
podman inspect -l
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install Podman v6.0.1.
2. Follow the official Getting Started guide.
3. Run:
```sh
podman inspect -l
```
4. Observe that the command fails.


### Describe the results you received

The command fails with:

```text
Error: unknown shorthand flag: 'l' in -l
See 'podman inspect --help'

### Describe the results you expected

Either:

- the documentation should use the currently supported command, or
- if `-l` is still intended to be supported, the command should work as documented.

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

- macOS 26.5.2
- Apple Silicon 


### Additional information

The outdated command appears on the official Getting Started documentation page.

Documentation page:
https://podman.io/docs

If this is intentional for an older version of Podman, it may be helpful to clarify the version the documentation applies to.

comments.body:
The documenation says:

> Note: The -l is a convenience argument for latest container. Instead of this short argument, you can also use the container's ID, container's name or the long argument (--latest).
> Note: If you are running remote Podman client, including Mac and Windows (excluding WSL2) machines, the -l option is not available.

The command is only available on Linux (`podman`), not on other platforms (`podman-remote`)
 
* #7127

```console
$ podman inspect --latest
[]
Error: no containers to inspect: no such container
$ podman-remote inspect --latest
Error: unknown flag: --latest
See 'podman-remote inspect --help'
```
I agree that this is clearly documented. Closing this, please feel free to reopen if you think there's more to be done.
